Phase Four Fixture

Abstract

This fixture exercises fall-backs added in phase four: complex tables that must be screenshot, sourceless MathML formulas, inline SVG diagrams, and adapter-based body selection. The prose is intentionally verbose so that the quality evaluator clears the text threshold even when image artifacts dominate.

1. Complex table (should fall back to screenshot)

The following table includes nested tables and many rowspans, pushing complexity score above 10.

Table 1: Hierarchical metrics.
GroupMacroNotes
PrecisionRecallF1
A0.910.900.905
subvalue
0.920.930.925cv stable
B0.880.860.870

2. Sourceless MathML formula

Inline scalar formula without any TeX annotation:

ab

Below is a MathJax-rendered placeholder that has no source (typical aftermath when JS has run):

3. Inline SVG diagram

4. Discussion

These constructs jointly stress the enrich stage: table complexity triggers image fallback, formula lacking TeX source also needs a screenshot, and inline SVG is preserved as-is. Additional prose here ensures the quality evaluator sees enough textual content.

We repeat: complex tables screenshot fallback, sourceless formula screenshot fallback, inline SVG inline preservation. This is a phase four regression artefact.

References

  1. Phase Four Fixture, 2026.
